spec_match.rationale: Horizontal scroll-snap with scroll-target-group is on-spec for the segmented-control pattern.

interactive.rationale: Zero interactive markers. Pure CSS demo — invariant 7 violation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises iOS-style switcher; CSS renders one, but visitor has nothing to drive.

edge_cases.rationale: Touch vs mouse vs keyboard? Visitor can't see — there's no comparison or fallback path.

openQuestions.title: Add keyboard arrow handling
openQuestions.detail: iOS segmented controls respond to arrow keys. Show that the new property + native anchors gives that for free vs a JS-driven implementation that has to wire roving tabindex.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit prev/next buttons
openQuestions.detail: Touch isn't always available. Buttons that scrollIntoView each segment would make the demo work on desktop without scroll friction.

summary: Conceptually elegant — segmented controls as TOCs is the right framing — but ships static. Needs keyboard/buttons to be a real demo.
